questions.question_text: CO Mapping Explain orthogonality with suitable example. Define Euclidean norm and Frobenius norm with suitable example. Find interquartile range for given dataset 2, 6, 9, 18, 12, 19, 27, 15, 7, 5, 1 If A= [ cos 𝜃 sin𝜃 −sin 𝜃 cos 𝜃] And A-1 = AT, Find the value of 𝜃 Draw a stem and leaf plot of the annual average daily minimum temperatures given below. 57.4, 34.1, 59.3, 51.0, 55.5, 48.1, 57.6, 49.0, 36.2, 39.5, 44.8, 49.2, 57.1, 69.0, 51.3, 70.0 What do you mean by independent and dependent events Explain binomial distribution with example. Explain various type of sampling. CO Mapping

subparts.text: Find out the relative frequency of the number of male who buys Sports car
Find out the relative frequency of the number of female who buys Sports Utility Vehicle 2
Find out the relative frequency of the number of females who buys both the types of car
Find out the relative frequency of both male and female buying Sports Utility Vehicle

questions.question_text: The following data relate to the different types of movies liked by 200 surveyed people. Find relative frequency and plot Pie Chart representing Types of Movies Liked by Peoples Types of Movies Comedy Action Romance Drama SciFi Animated Number of People Liking 42 50 55 9 32 12 CO Mapping
Consider the matrix for some variable ‘a’. Find all values of ‘a’ which will prove that A has eigenvalues 0, 3, and −3.
Consider input vectors in a set X as X1 = [ 1 3 4 ] X2 = [ 2 4 1 ] and Two other vectors z1 = [ −3 −5 2 ] z2 = [ 3 7 1 ] Show that z1 is linearly dependent and z2 is linearly independent CO Mapping

A box contains 4 red marbles, 3 green marbles and 2 blue marbles. What is the probability that the first marble is blue and the second marble is green if: 1. One marble is removed from the box and then replaced and then another marble is drawn from the box 2. One marble is removed from the box and it is not replaced and then another marble is drawn from the box.

If electricity power failures occur according to a Poisson distribution with an average of 3 failures every 20 weeks, calculate the probability that there will not be more than one failure during a particular week. CO Mapping
Explain Linear classification problems with suitable example
